description: The histogram uses drastically unequal bin widths — some bins span just 1 unit (e.g., 0-1, 1-2) while the last bin spans 40 units (10-50) — yet all bars are displayed at equal visual widths. This makes the "10-50" bin appear comparable in height to narrower bins, masking the fact that it covers a range 40 times wider; a reader would incorrectly perceive the distribution as more uniform than it actually is, since the wide bins artificially concentrate sparse high-value data into visually prominent bars.
